title: Knowledge Distillation in LLMs
concept: Knowledge distillation trains a smaller 'student' model to mimic the probability distributions and behaviors of a large 'teacher' model.

exercises.prompt: Why do we want to perform knowledge distillation on large language models?

options.text: To make models smaller and faster
To make models larger and slower

exercises.explain: Knowledge distillation transfers knowledge from a large model to a smaller one, making AI applications faster and more accessible.

cards.title: Shrinking Giant AI Brains
cards.body: Giant AI models are incredibly smart, but they are too slow and expensive to run on your phone. How do we shrink these digital giants without losing their brilliant minds? We make them teach a student!

cards.title: What is Knowledge Distillation?
cards.body: Knowledge Distillation is a technique where a smaller, faster AI model (the 'student') is trained to reproduce the outputs and reasoning patterns of a larger, smarter AI model (the 'teacher'). This helps pack giant AI brains into smaller, everyday devices.

cards.title: The Master Chef and Apprentice
cards.body: Imagine a master chef (the teacher) who knows hundreds of complex recipes. Instead of writing a massive cookbook, the chef shows an apprentice (the student) exactly how they taste-test and balance flavors. By copying the chef's decision-making style, the apprentice learns to cook like a master without needing years of trial and error.

cards.title: Distillation in Action
cards.body: A massive model with 70 billion parameters (the teacher) calculates that the word 'bark' is 80% likely to refer to a dog and 20% likely to refer to a tree in a specific sentence. A smaller 7 billion parameter model (the student) is trained to predict those exact same percentages, learning the subtle nuances of language much faster.

cards.title: Myth: Just a Copycat
cards.body: People think a student model trained through distillation can only copy-paste exact answers. In reality, because the student learns the probability distribution (the 'why' behind the answers), it generalizes incredibly well to completely new questions it has never seen before.

cards.title: Teacher vs. Student
cards.body: Let's compare the roles of the two models during the knowledge distillation process.

right.text: Small, fast, cheap to run. Learns to match the teacher's outputs to become highly capable.

left.text: Large, slow, expensive, but highly capable. Generates rich probability distributions for the student to study.

exercises.prompt: What key information does the teacher model share with the student model during knowledge distillation?

options.text: Its probability distributions and reasoning behaviors
Its exact physical size and parameter count

exercises.explain: The teacher model shares its full probability distribution (often called 'soft targets' or 'dark knowledge'), which contains rich information about how it ranks different possibilities.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The primary goal of knowledge distillation is to make a model that is smaller and faster to run.
exercises.explain: True. The main benefit of distillation is creating a smaller, faster student model that can run efficiently on smaller hardware like phones or laptops while retaining high accuracy.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the description of model roles.
exercises.sentence: During distillation, the smaller model acting as the student learns to mimic the larger model acting as the teacher.
exercises.explain: In knowledge distillation, the student model learns by trying to mimic the behaviors and probability outputs of the larger teacher model.

exercises.prompt:
Which source acts as an AI teacher providing 'distillation' knowledge?
Model A: 'For this sentence, "Apple" has a 70% chance of being the fruit and a 30% chance of being the company.'
Model B: 'The correct label is 1 (Fruit). All other options are 0.'
exercises.explain: Model A is the AI because it outputs a distribution of probabilities (soft labels) which allows the student to learn *why* options are related, whereas Model B only gives a rigid correct/incorrect binary label.

exercises.prompt: You want to deploy a fast LLM on a smartphone while keeping the reasoning quality of a giant supercomputer model. Which approach best describes how you would use Knowledge Distillation?

options.text: It combines the weights of two identical models to double their size
It forces the student model to memorize the exact training data of the teacher
It trains a smaller student model to mimic the probability distributions of a larger teacher model
It deletes random parts of a large model to make it run faster

exercises.explain: Knowledge distillation transfers the dark knowledge (soft probabilities) of a teacher model to a student, allowing the smaller model to achieve high performance with a fraction of the size.
